nodes.label: S7 Order Detail (Salesman) — History, Disputes, Edit/Cancel
nodes.description: Order detail screen for the salesman role, showing four lifecycle states on one sheet: editable (countdown running), locked (read-only after window expiry or accountant processing), processed (terminal read-only), and cancelled (with confirm dialog). Line items are immutable snapshots — catalog price changes never rewrite history. Events shown in plain-words history log.

nodes.label: S7-a Editable State — Countdown Running

line_items.name: Micro Usb Cable MU240 - ZB CABLE (White)

history: Edited 12:05 — Micro Usb Cable MU240 qty 8→10
nodes.footnote: Edit reopens S4 pre-filled with these quantities. Prices shown are the submit-time snapshot.

nodes.label: S7-b Locked State — Read-Only
nodes.line_items_display: 6 lines — collapsed in this state card
nodes.banner: The edit window has ended. Call the accountant to change this order.
nodes.footnote: Locked is derived: window expiry OR the accountant processed it — the chip reads the same either way. Edit/Cancel buttons are gone, not disabled.

nodes.label: S7-c Processed State — Terminal Read-Only
history: Edited after lock today 10:55 — TYPE C TO TYPE C CABLE TT27 PLUS (BLACK) qty 5→10 · reason: shop called
nodes.banner: Booked into Tally by the office. For any change, call the accountant.
nodes.footnote: The salesman goes read-only the instant the accountant processes — even mid-countdown. After-lock edits appear here in plain words, so shop-side disputes die quickly.

nodes.label: S7-d Cancelled State + Confirm Dialog
nodes.footnote: Salesman cancel = confirm only (no reason needed while editable). Accountant cancel always logs a reason — see S9.

nodes.label: Cancel Order Confirm Dialog
nodes.title: Cancel this order?
nodes.body:
ORD-2026-1041 · Mobile Point · ₹1,255
This can't be undone.
nodes.design_note: Destructive confirm — no reason field required for salesman cancellation while order is editable

nodes.label: Edit Window Countdown Timer (Status Chip)
nodes.purpose: Shows remaining time in the salesman edit window. When window expires (or accountant processes), chip transitions to 'Submitted · locked' and action buttons disappear.

nodes.label: Plain-Words History / Event Log
nodes.source_location: HISTORY section present in all four state panels
nodes.purpose: Append-only plain-language audit trail. Records: submission, edits (with before/after quantities and reason when post-lock), window expiry, processing by accountant. Visible to salesman to enable dispute resolution without calling office.
nodes.design_principle: Plain words — no codes or jargon; after-lock edits show qty diff and reason so shop-side disputes die quickly

nodes.label: Immutable Line Items Snapshot
nodes.source_location: Line items section in S7-a panel; collapsed in S7-b
nodes.decision: Line items are a snapshot of what was ordered at submit time — catalog price changes never rewrite history. Prices shown are submit-time snapshot, not current catalog prices.
nodes.implication: Guarantees billing accuracy and immutable order record; critical for dispute resolution
